title: APC Network Management Card SNMP Communication Loss
dataSources: Requires Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) or the SNMP Modular Input add-on (Splunkbase #1537). Primary poll metrics arrive as sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups with the extracted numeric field upsCommStatus from PowerNet-MIB; complementary APC enterprise SNMP traps may appear under sourcetype=snmp:trap carrying communicationLost semantics for the same hosts.
app: Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266), SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537)
spl:
index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=*
| eval comm=tonumber(upsCommStatus)
| stats latest(comm) as upsCommStatus by host
| where upsCommStatus=2
description: The PowerNet-MIB upsCommStatus counter tells you whether the APC Network Management Card still has live SNMP to the UPS electronics. When upsCommStatus reads commLost (classic mapping uses integer 2), your Splunk SNMP poll or trap stream loses telemetry even though utility power at the rack may look fine—so operators cannot trust remaining runtime or load segments until communications recover.
value: You gain early warning before a UPS becomes an invisible single point of failure: teams can dispatch facilities or NetOps while power is still stable, correlate the loss against switch or ACL changes, and avoid discovering the blind spot only after load shed or battery exhaustion.
implementation: Baseline every APC firmware train because upsCommStatus enumerations occasionally shift between maintenance releases—promote lookups only after snmpget proof. When traps are sparse, dual-path ingest (poll + trap) prevents silent gaps if UDP traps drop during congestion. Pair Splunk alerts with ICMP tests sourced from two subnets so asymmetric routing failures do not masquerade as dead UPS hardware.
visualization: Primary single-value panel for distinct APC hosts currently at upsCommStatus=2; sortable table with host, mgmt_ip, last OK time, and correlated NetOps ticket; timeline overlay showing trap bursts versus poll gaps; optional choropleth if mgmt_ip geo metadata exists for campus fleets.

references.title: APC FAQ — SNMP management card troubleshooting
Splunk Connect for SNMP documentation (GitHub Pages)
Splunk Connect for SNMP on Splunkbase
SNMP Modular Input add-on on Splunkbase

knownFalsePositives:
• APC Network Management Card firmware upgrades initiated from APC Local Management Interface during change windows—suppress by joining Splunk results to CMDB maintenance tags or paused alert macros for the maintenance window UUID.
• Layer-2 switch spanning-tree topology changes on the access VLAN carrying SNMP—distinguish when interface counters show TCN bursts within two minutes of upsCommStatus spikes while icmp_ping from an alternate router still succeeds.
• Heavy Forwarder recycling the SNMP Modular Input after systemd restarts—distinguish when every sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host shows simultaneous commLost keyed to the same forwarder hostname in splunk_server metadata.
• SNMPv3 authentication lockouts after mistyped privacy keys—distinguish when Splunk internal logs show snmpv3 handshake failures while upsCommStatus drops only for hosts sharing that credential profile.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Deploy Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) workers or configure the SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537) on a Heavy Forwarder that can reach UDP/161 to each APC Network Management Card; confirm RBAC so splunkd can reload inputs—typically admin on Enterprise or sc_admin approvals on Splunk Cloud before publishing HEC or modular-input secrets.
• Reserve index=power (or your contracted SNMP landing index) and enforce props/transforms so sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups retains upsCommStatus as a numeric field; mirror trap ingest for APC enterprise OID .1.3.6.1.4.1.318 into sourcetype=snmp:trap when polls alone would miss asynchronous communicationLost events.
• Poll upsCommStatus at least every five minutes and document the OID-to-integer mapping per firmware family (commonly 1=ok, 2=commLost on classic builds).

Configuration steps
1. From a bastion host run snmpwalk/snmpget against a pilot UPS to capture live upsCommStatus samples; stash the proof in change records before Splunk alerting goes live.
2. Validate modular inputs via REST GET /services/data/inputs/snmp (or the equivalent SC4SNMP inventory UI) so each mgmt_ip maps to the intended sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups stanza without duplicate community strings shadowing each other.
3. Layer NetOps correlation: join hosts that flip upsCommStatus=2 with recent interfaceDown or firewall-change knowledge objects so VLAN ACL regressions separate from dead NMC hardware.
4. Tune scheduled searches with dispatch.earliest_time=-15m and three consecutive poll failures before paging to absorb transient SNMP timeouts during brief spanning-tree reconvergence.
5. Stand up a redundant poller host so scheduled maintenance on one collector never marks every UPS commLost simultaneously.

Operational follow-through
• Normalize odd string extractions using case() when legacy TA versions emit textual status tokens.
• Capture weekly CSV snapshots from the comm-loss saved search into the facilities evidence share for audit replay.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Lab APC Network Management Card has SNMP blocked at the nearest switch ACL while utility feed stays up; sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups samples show upsCommStatus holding numeric 2 across three consecutive five-minute polls and optional sourcetype=snmp:trap emits communicationLost for the same mgmt_ip.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Operators bounce only the Splunk Heavy Forwarder service; every UPS simultaneously logs upsCommStatus=2 for one poll cycle yet snmpget from a laptop still returns upsCommStatus=1, proving collector downtime rather than APC communications loss.

evidence: Scheduled saved search archive of hosts whose latest upsCommStatus equals 2, exported weekly to the restricted facilities evidence library with Splunk audit fields (_time, host, mgmt_ip) retained for 400 days.
exclusions: Sites that refuse SNMP toward APC cards, environments monitored solely through local serial consoles without Splunk ingest, or racks whose power gear never exposes PowerNet-MIB upsCommStatus to Splunk collectors.
